questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)?

options.text: To develop web browsers and operating systems
To act as a central governing body for the Internet
To provide internet connection services globally
To develop protocols and guidelines that ensure long-term growth for the Web

questions.explanationMd: The W3C is an international community where member organizations, a full-time staff, and the public work together to develop Web standards.

questions.questionMd: In the context of the WWW, what is hypertext?

options.text: A protocol for securely transferring files between computers
A database format used to store website settings
A programming language used to style web page layouts
Text that contains links to other texts or documents

questions.explanationMd: Hypertext is text displayed on a computer display or other electronic devices with references (hyperlinks) to other text that the reader can immediately access.

questions.questionMd: What is the role of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language) in relation to HTML?

options.text: SGML is a style sheet language used to format HTML documents.
HTML was completely replaced by SGML in modern web development.
SGML is a standard for creating markup languages, and early versions of HTML were based on it.
SGML is a scripting language that runs in the browser.

questions.explanationMd: SGML is the standard from which early versions of HTML were derived. It defines the rules for creating markup languages.

questions.questionMd: Which tag must be used as the root element of an HTML document?

options.text: <head>
<body>
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

questions.explanationMd: The `<html>` element is the root element of an HTML page, containing all other elements.

questions.questionMd: How do LANs and WANs relate to the concepts of Intranet and Internet, respectively?

options.text: LANs form private internal networks (Intranets), while WANs connect these networks together to form the Internet
LANs are strictly used for the Internet, while WANs are strictly used for Intranets
LANs operate over wireless networks, while WANs operate over wired networks
Both LANs and WANs are entirely isolated from the global internet

questions.explanationMd: Local Area Networks (LANs) typically make up private corporate Intranets, while Wide Area Networks (WANs) connect these smaller networks globally to form the Internet.

questions.questionMd: Which statement is TRUE regarding the history of HTTP/2?

options.text: HTTP/2 requires encrypted connections by default.
HTTP/2 removed all headers used in HTTP/1.1.
HTTP/2 was designed to overcome performance limitations of HTTP/1.1 without changing its semantics.
HTTP/2 only supports text-based communication, not binary.

questions.explanationMd: HTTP/2 introduced features like multiplexing to speed up page loads without altering the core semantics (methods, status codes, URIs) of HTTP.

questions.questionMd: The Internet was initially created by which organization, under what name, and for what primary purpose?

options.text: European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN)
World Wide Web Consortium (W3C)
Department of Defense's Advanced Research Projects Agency (ARPA); ARPANET; military communication and research
The Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF)

questions.explanationMd: The Internet began as ARPANET, funded by the US Department of Defense's ARPA, to build a robust, fault-tolerant communication network.

questions.questionMd: Who invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN?

options.text: Steve Jobs
Tim Berners-Lee
Bill Gates
Alan Turing

questions.explanationMd: Sir Tim Berners-Lee is credited with inventing the World Wide Web while working at CERN in 1989.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a primary goal of CSS?

options.text: To add interactive logic to web pages
To query databases for web data
To separate the presentation of a document from its structure and content
To define the HTML tags on a page

questions.explanationMd: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) was designed to separate document content (HTML) from document presentation (layout, colors, fonts).

questions.questionMd: Which HTTP method is considered "idempotent" by default?

options.text: POST
PATCH
GET
CONNECT

questions.explanationMd: An idempotent HTTP method is one that can be called many times without producing different outcomes. GET is idempotent; it simply retrieves data.

questions.questionMd: In web development, what is a "port"?

options.text: A physical connection defining hardware interfaces
A function that holds an IP address
A communication endpoint mapped to a specific process or network service
A standard URL parameter

questions.explanationMd: A port is a virtual point where network connections start and end. It is managed by the OS and helps direct traffic to the correct application.

questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://www.example.com:8080`, what does the `8080` represent?

options.text: The TCP port number the server is listening on
The subnet mask of the server IP address
The file size allocated for the document request
The maximum payload size of the HTTP request

questions.explanationMd: The number following the colon after the hostname in a URL specifies the port number on which the server is listening for traffic.

questions.questionMd: How is a static method defined on a class in modern JS?

options.text: By using the `static` keyword before the method name inside the class body
By using the `@static` decorator before the class definition
By placing the method outside of the class block
By listing them as parameters in the class constructor line

questions.explanationMd: In JavaScript, you define static methods by prepending the `static` keyword to the method name within a class definition.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary benefit of the `<canvas>` element in HTML5?

options.text: It acts as a layout container for multiple HTML components at the same time
It provides a responsive iframe to embed other websites easily
It handles complex form submissions securely without a server
It provides a drawing surface that is manipulated via JavaScript for rendering 2D and 3D graphics

questions.explanationMd: The `<canvas>` element acts as a container for graphics, requiring a script (usually JavaScript) to draw the graphics programmatically.

questions.questionMd: What happens to the variables declared inside a function when the function finishes executing (assuming no closures)?

options.text: They are immediately garbage collected after the outer function has finished
They are kept alive forever just in case the function is called again
They are saved to LocalStorage by the browser
They become global variables accessible anywhere in the program

questions.explanationMd: Local variables within a function scope are generally cleared from memory (garbage collected) once the function's execution context ends, unless a closure keeps a reference to them.
